decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at its upper bound of 500 W/(m*K). Since material tuning is maxed out, the next best lever is to increase the geometric size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain width (0.25 + 0.84 = 1.09 < 1.2).

changes.reason: Increasing spreader width expands the lateral area for heat dissipation, reducing thermal resistance from the chip to the boundaries.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip maximum temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer.
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the right boundary; ensure it does not exceed the design domain width in future steps.
Diminishing returns may occur if the spreader becomes too wide relative to the heat source location.
